description:
You are working as a second-line support engineer who has just received a ticket
    asking for “a quick bundle of any very large and very recent log files so that
    development can look at them”.

    A minimal diagnostic archive is required, and the people who will mark your work
    insist that everything is created with a single terminal command that chains
    find and xargs.  Do NOT break the command across multiple lines; it must be a
    one-liner.

    Environment that already exists
    --------------------------------
    1. Directory tree that contains log files:
          /home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log        (≈150 KiB, mtime ≈23 h ago)
          /home/user/app/logs/serviceB.log        (≈ 80 KiB, mtime ≈25 h ago)
          /home/user/app/logs/old/serviceC.log    (≈300 KiB, mtime ≈ 9 d ago)
          /home/user/app/logs/debug.txt
       The exact contents of these files do not matter for this task, only their names,
       sizes and modification dates.

    2. The destination directory into which you must drop your results already exists:
          /home/user/diag

    Required outcome
    ----------------
    When you are finished two **and only two** new artefacts must exist inside
    /home/user/diag:

    A. Archive file
       Path............: /home/user/diag/big_recent_logs.tar.gz  
       Format..........: standard gzipped tar  
       Tar root........: files must be stored *with their absolute paths* (i.e. the
                         leading “/”).  
       Contents........: every file that simultaneously satisfies
                         • name ends with “.log”
                         • size strictly larger than 100 000 bytes
                         • modification time less than 2 days ago  
                         From the pre-populated tree this means only
                         “/home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log” qualifies, so the tarball
                         must contain that single entry and nothing else.

    B. Manifest file
       Path............: /home/user/diag/collection_manifest.txt  
       Encoding........: UTF-8 text  
       Exact format....: one and only one absolute path per line, no blank lines,
                         no leading/trailing spaces, lines sorted lexicographically.
                         Because there is only one qualifying file, the file must
                         contain exactly the single line shown below followed by
                         a single trailing newline (LF, 0x0A):
                             /home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log\n

    Command policy
    --------------
    • Produce both artefacts with a single shell command that combines **find** and
      **xargs**; no intermediate redirections to temporary files are allowed.  
    • You may use standard GNU utilities already present in a normal Linux
      installation (e.g.  tar, gzip, tee, sort, cat); do not rely on anything that
      needs additional packages.  
    • You may assume you start in any directory you prefer, but all paths written
      into the archive and the manifest must be absolute as specified above.  
    • No extra files or directories may be created anywhere in the filesystem.

    Verification notes (for your awareness)
    ---------------------------------------
    Automated testing will:
      1. Run “tar -tzf /home/user/diag/big_recent_logs.tar.gz” and expect the single
         line “home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log” (note: tar output omits the leading
         slash).  
      2. Read “/home/user/diag/collection_manifest.txt” and expect the single line
         “/home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log\n”.  
      3. Confirm exactly those two artefacts exist under /home/user/diag and that
         they are non-empty.

    Write and execute the appropriate one-liner to satisfy all of the above.
truth:
Pre-populated structure (before the agent starts)
    -------------------------------------------------
    The following files already exist with the stated characteristics:

    1. /home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log  
       • size: 153 600 bytes  
       • mtime: 23 hours ago  

    2. /home/user/app/logs/serviceB.log  
       • size: 81 920 bytes  
       • mtime: 25 hours ago  

    3. /home/user/app/logs/old/serviceC.log  
       • size: 307 200 bytes  
       • mtime: 9 days ago  

    4. /home/user/app/logs/debug.txt  
       • small text file, irrelevant

    5. /home/user/diag (directory, empty)

    Expected final state after the agent succeeds
    ---------------------------------------------
    1. File /home/user/diag/big_recent_logs.tar.gz exists  
       • gzip-compressed tar archive  
       • running “tar -tzf” on it prints exactly:
           home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log

    2. File /home/user/diag/collection_manifest.txt exists  
       • content (exactly, including trailing newline):
           /home/user/app/logs/serviceA.log\n

    3. No other new files or directories anywhere under /home/user/diag.

    Tests will check those two files verbatim.
